seo.title: Context Window Management for AI Agents in Haystack Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix context window management for ai agents in Haystack agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Haystack, Context Window Management issues for AI Agents arise when pipelines exceed LLM token limits during RAG flows, causing truncation or failures in components like DocumentStores and Generators. This manifests as silent errors in Pipeline.connect() chains where retrieved documents overwhelm the prompt builder. Agents built with Haystack's Agent framework amplify this when tool calls chain multiple retrievals without token tracking.

howItManifests.symptom: Generator receives 200k+ tokens after DocumentRetriever fetches top-50 docs
howItManifests.impact: LLM API 400 errors or truncated responses losing critical context

howItManifests.symptom: Cumulative context from sequential tool calls exceeds 128k token limit
howItManifests.impact: Agent loops indefinitely or hallucinates due to incomplete prompt

howItManifests.symptom: No token counting before pipeline.run() causing overflow
High top_k returns long docs without metadata filtering
howItManifests.impact: Pipeline crashes with MemoryError during prompt assembly

detection.name: Token Overflow Monitor
detection.description: Custom component that measures input tokens before Generator using tiktoken

detection.name: Pipeline Response Length Check
Document Length Distribution
detection.description: Retriever wrapper logging document token stats
detection.code:
from haystack.components.retrievers import InMemoryEmbeddingRetriever
import numpy as np

class TokenAwareRetriever(InMemoryEmbeddingRetriever):
    def run(self, query_embedding, top_k=5):
        result = super().run(query_embedding=query_embedding, top_k=top_k)
        docs = result['documents']
        encoder = tiktoken.encoding_for_model('gpt-4')
        lengths = [len(encoder.encode(d.content)) for d in docs]
        print(f"Doc lengths: mean={np.mean(lengths):.0f}, max={max(lengths)}")
        return result

fixes.name: Dynamic Top-K Adjuster
fixes.description: Retriever component that adapts top_k based on avg doc length

fixes.name: Context Truncation Component
fixes.description: Ranks and summarizes docs to fit token budget

fixes.name: Token-Budget Pipeline
fixes.description: Full pipeline with enforced token limits
fixes.code:
from haystack import Pipeline

pipeline = Pipeline()
pipeline.add_component("retriever", InMemoryEmbeddingRetriever())
pipeline.add_component("truncator", ContextTruncator(max_tokens=90000))
pipeline.add_component("generator", OpenAIChatGenerator())

pipeline.connect("retriever.documents", "truncator.documents")
pipeline.connect("truncator.documents", "generator.documents")

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Context Window Management for AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates as a Haystack custom component that automatically instruments all pipelines for token overflow detection using real-time tiktoken counting and LLM API response monitoring. It captures Pipeline.run() traces, measures document retrieval token costs, and flags when Generator inputs exceed 90% of model limits across OpenAI, Anthropic, and custom providers. Vex's agent learns failure patterns specific to your DocumentStore and retriever combinations, auto-generating adaptive top_k values and truncation strategies.

Remediation runs synthetic queries against your ElasticsearchDocumentStore or InMemory store, validating fixes before deployment. Vex dashboards show token utilization heatmaps per pipeline branch and automatic PRs with ContextTruncator components. For Haystack Agents, Vex traces multi-tool call sequences to prevent cumulative overflow across retrieval chains.
vexAutomation.code:
from haystack import Pipeline
from vex_sdk.haystack import VexMonitor

# Install: pip install haystack-ai vex-sdk

rag_pipeline = Pipeline()

# Vex auto-instruments token counting
vex_monitor = VexMonitor(
    model='gpt-4',
    max_tokens=128000,
    failure_threshold=0.9  # Alert at 90%
)

rag_pipeline.add_component('vex', vex_monitor)
rag_pipeline.add_component('retriever', InMemoryEmbeddingRetriever())
rag_pipeline.add_component('generator', OpenAIChatGenerator())

# Vex auto-connects to all document flows
rag_pipeline.connect('retriever.documents', 'vex.documents')
rag_pipeline.connect('vex.documents', 'generator.documents')

# Vex runs validation suite
vex_monitor.validate_pipeline(rag_pipeline, test_queries=['sample query'])

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Add one line of Vex to your Haystack pipeline and eliminate context window failures forever.
